ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: K'a gɔɔta an nɩ meer bam bɩ, karɩndanyɩnɔ bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ gʊaa deem Zidas bɩ lee ku. Bʊr a ʊ kan zamaa cir gʊta kɩ, ŋn'ɩ kargʊrɔ m kan bənkənno kɩ. Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ kan zɩɩfʊrɔ gʊɔɔgusinno wɔɔ kɩ yɩ ŋ nyɔɔ.
translated: The dog's mother was very surprised to see her child come back home with a Zidas bag on his back. She asked him where he got the bag from, and he told her that he bought it. The woman was suspicious and asked him how he got the money, and he told her that he sold a goat to buy it.

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n ta, ŋnɩ hɔ kʊ Yeezuu a hɩ ŋ nɩ bɩ ba.
translated: The people were baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Yeezuu k'a naa do dɩnda haay bɩ, a a hʊr Galilee ʊ, an ta Zidee ʊ, Zʊrdɛn kur.
translated: Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan River.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: Herod therefore sent them to Bethlehem and ordered them: “Go and find out everything about the child; then report back to me, so that I too may go and offer my homage to him.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Kʊ ŋ higʊta bɩ zɛ ra bɩ, ŋ yaa lee ku Zenezarɛtɩ ʊ.
translated: Go and announce to the people, you have heard it from Zenesarati.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Lʊ nɩ b'ʊ an ta Yeezuu zi, zɩɩfʊ bɛɛ nɩ y, Kanaan do nyɩ m, an a hɩ Yeezuu m, a ʊ: «M cicir zɛ Zuuba, *Davɩd Nyɩ, mɔɔ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ zinə n ta m, an nɩ fɩr kam ma gʊta.»
translated: He entered a house and again the crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples could not even eat a meal. When his own people heard of this, they went out to take custody of him, for they were saying, «He has lost his senses."

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedee nyɩnɔ da a zɔ Yeezuu ma kan a nyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ, an kukurə zɛ a taa, k'a hɔ yɛ han.
translated: Zebedee's daughter and her mother were there with Jesus, and they were mourning and wailing.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Karɩndanyɩ a ga m, an yɩ am a karɩnsaamba m. Zibəənyɩ a ga m, an yɩ am a zuuba m. Kʊ n har bɩ zaa bɩ ba zinəro jɩtaarɛ, awɔɔ hɔɔn ʊ naa, n b'a bam har bɩ nyɩnɔ wɔɔ m ncɩnaaʊ ra? (Likki
translated: What do you want to eat, you are eating karinsaamba. What do you want to drink, you are drinking zuuba. You are eating everything you want, you are drinking everything you want. Is it good that your mother works hard, your father works hard, and you are just sitting down enjoying (the fruits of their labor), isn't it?

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: A a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Ŋ bɩ hɔsɩ sa a wɔ ʊ zaa sarɛ minto y. Bur, hɛra, busoo, a b'a sa y, k'a maam bara m.
translated: When you are sick, you say: «The sickness has made me like this, it has taken my strength. My head, my body, everywhere it hurts, I am not feeling well."

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: A bɩ hɩra sa zaa bɩ sarɛ minto, b'a bɩ huu sa hɩɩya y. A bɩ kʊsɔ kan bara kɩ sa y, bala, zibəəzaa b'a ga m, an hɔbɩrɛ yɩ.
translated: If you want to get knowledge, you must be willing to hurt your body. You must make your body get tired, because learning is hard work.

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa yɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Dɩga ɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ ma, hɔ k'a n a ga barɛ m *sabaa hinni ʊ bɩ ŋn'a bam.»
translated: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to test Jesus. They asked him to show them a sign from heaven. He answered: «You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but you cannot interpret the signs of the times. An evil and adulterous generation seeks a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.»

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Zaan k'a nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ b'a to an Krista zibəəro wɔɔ mim ma, an a karɩndanyɩnɔ gɔsɩnnɔ nyɔɔ,
translated: When you hear the good news about Christ that brings life, you must not turn a deaf ear to it.

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: K'a ser bɩ yɩ, a heer yɩm nyɩnta kan kɩ an lɛ da bunɛɛhʊ a yaa nɛɛhʊ rɔ kʊ ŋ n nyaam nɔ wɔɔ ra.
translated: If you want to eat, you go to your mother's place and you cry for food, then she will give you food to eat.

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: K'a dundo a zi kan ŋʊaarlɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ kɩ, bʊr ŋ tɩr a ʊ awɔɔ zi, am seero hɔ bɩ m, an bɔkarɛ poojino kʊsɩrɔ m.
translated: The dog chases the person who steals things from him, but he does not catch him, he runs and gets tired, then he stops and gets angry.

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Bala, Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛrɛ a Zɩ lɛbɔʊrɛ nɔ ʊ kan a malɛɛkarɔ kɩ. Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, a gʊaa biyəə zibəə nyɔɔdʊ kaŋ ʊ.»
translated: “Friend, I want to tell you a story about a man who was very lazy. His friends advised him, but he didn’t listen to them.”

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yeezuu k'a nɩ meer bam kan gʊɔɔ cir bɩ kɩ bɩ, a da kan a danyɩnɔ kɩ bʊr, ŋnɩ jɩm garga ra, ŋn'a ŋʊam k'a meer ba kan kɩ. [
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, "You will all be scattered, each one to his own home, and you will leave me alone; but I am not alone, for the Father is with me."

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu meer ba kan ŋ kɩ, a ʊ: «K'a sɩra ka, mɔɔ m, a da bɩ bɔ ʊ y!»
translated: Go and say to Jesus: «If you want, you can heal me!»

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a ta!» Ŋ nɩ bɔ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ mɛ ʊ, ŋnɩ gasʊ kurkuuro wɔɔ mɛ ʊ. A m bɩ, kurkuuro cir gʊta bɩ n bra si, ŋnɩ zer, ŋn'a zar higʊta b'ʊ, ŋnɩ gɩnga.
translated: The mother said to him: «Go there!» He went there and found a woman, she was pounding millet. He saw her, the millet pestle was very long, he liked it, he took it and ran away with it, and threw it.

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Bɩ, lɛɛkarɛzannɔ gʊta nyɩntam jɛzannɔ, jɛzannɔ gʊta n nyɩnta lɛɛkarɛzannɔ.»
translated: The one who was a stranger becomes a citizen, and the citizen becomes a stranger.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: A a ya, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «N bɩr sɛ dam fɩtɩrɛ ʊ, n n'a cɩntam kasɩ gɛɛ gargʊ taa y, gɛɛ lɔ? A cɩnta n nawʊm hɔ burə ʊ.
translated: What is this, I am seeing here, you? You are making a big noise here, you are not ashamed, are you? You are making noise at my compound.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Cir bɩ n zibəənyɩ bɩ bir an a hɩ m, a ʊ: ‹Zibəənyɩ bʊnyaa, mɔɔ ɩ zɩm bɩ to ɩbɩɩ m haay, k'ɩbɩɩ cicir ba mɔɔ lɛ ra bɩ minto.
translated: The person who does not know how to farm, does not know how to eat. Zibəənyɩ is a farm, if you do not cultivate it well, it will not produce anything, if you do not weed it, it will not produce.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: an a sɩŋŋɩda sa naa ma, awɔɔ b'a yarɛ a mɔɔ yɩ y, k'awɔɔ m b'a hɩ, a ʊ: ‹Barka ɩ gʊaa k'a nɩ zɛm Zuuba tɔ ma bɩ mim ʊ!› »
translated: He said to her while she was pounding: "My mother, I have a small matter, if you are not busy, I will tell you, and if you are busy, I will go. ‹Thank you, I have brought you some Zuuba sauce to eat with your rice!›"

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

translated: The God of heaven whom I worship, the one Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob worshipped, He will give you a reward.
